chapter_title.ar: باب أَكْلِ مَا يَسْقُطُ مِنَ الْخِوَانِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – Eat what falls from the food-spread

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/117)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/117)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَنْ وَجَدَ كِسْرَةً فَأَكَلَهَا كَانَتْ لَهُ حَسَنَةٌ وَ مَنْ وَجَدَهَا فِي قَذِرٍ فَغَسَلَهَا ثُمَّ رَفَعَهَا كَانَتْ لَهُ سَبْعُونَ حَسَنَةً .

en.hubeali: Humeyd Bin Ziyad, from Al Khashaab, from Ibn Baqqa, from Amro Bin Jumi’a who said,

‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The one who finds one crumb, so he eats it, would have a Reward for him; and the one who finds it in dirt, so he washes it, then raises it, would have seventy Rewards for him’.
en.sarwar: 5. Humayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Khashshab from ibn Baqqah from ‘Amr ibn Jami’ who has said the following: “He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has stated this Hadith. ‘The Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, “If one finds pieces of food and eats them it is one good deed, and if one finds them in unclean places, then washes them, then picks them up, he will have seventy good deeds recorded for him.’””
